title: woodsia pilosa
intro: Woodsia pilosa, also known as the hair fern, is a species of fern native to North America and Asia, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Woodsia pilosa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to promote overall well-being.
